subject: Chemistry
solution:
The kinetic energy of the ejected electron is given by the equation
$$
\begin{aligned}
h v &=h v_0+\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \quad \because v=\frac{c}{\lambda} \\
& \text { or } \quad \frac{h c}{\lambda} &=\frac{h c}{\lambda_0}+\frac{1}{2} m v^2 \\
\frac{1}{2} m v^2 &=\frac{h c}{\lambda}-\frac{h c}{\lambda_0} \\
&=h c\left(\frac{\lambda_0-\lambda}{\lambda \lambda_0}\right) \\
\therefore \quad & v^2 &=\frac{2 h c}{m}\left(\frac{\left.\lambda_0-\lambda\right)}{\lambda \lambda_0}\right) \\
& & v &=\sqrt{\frac{2 h c}{m}\left(\frac{\lambda_0-\lambda}{\lambda \lambda_0}\right)}
\end{aligned}
$$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For n=1   value of l=0, 1, 2

For n=2   value of l=0, 1, 2, 3

So, according to n+l rule the filling order of subshells will be :

1s 1p 2s 1d 2p 3s 2d 3p 4s.....

(1) 1st alkali metal will have configuration 1s2 1p6 so atomic number will be 8.

(2) 1st alkali metal will have electronic configuration ⇒   1s1   ⇒  Z=1

(3) Electronic configuration of CZ=6  ⇒  1s2 1p4

(4) Z=13, Electronic configuration =1s2 1p6 2s2 1d3

So it will not have half-filled electronic configuration.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Total energy of a revolving electron is the sum of its kinetic and potential energy. Total energy $=$ K.E. $+$ P. E.
$$
=\frac{e^2}{2 r}+\left(-\frac{e^2}{r}\right)=-\frac{e^2}{2 r}
$$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The Lyman series lies in the ultraviolet, in chemistry, the Lyman series is a hydrogen spectral series of transitions and resulting ultraviolet emission lines of the hydrogen atom as an electron goes from n≥2 to n=1  (where n is the principal quantum number), the lowest energy level of the electron.

All of the wavelengths in the Balmer series are in the visible region of the electromagnetic spectrum (400 nm to 740 nm). The presence of Hydrogen is detected in astronomy by using the H-Alpha line of the Balmer series; it is also a component of the solar spectrum.

All the wavelength of the Paschen series falls in the Infrared region of the electromagnetic spectrum.

Pfund series lie in the infrared.

A – II, B – IV, C – III, D – I

subject: Chemistry
solution:
For a charged particle accelerated through potential V, the de Broglie wavelength is $\lambda = \frac{h}{\sqrt{2mVe}}$.
The wavelength is inversely proportional to $\sqrt{m}$.
Therefore: $\frac{\lambda_1}{\lambda_2} = \sqrt{\frac{m_2}{m_1}} = \sqrt{\frac{4}{1}} = 2$.
This means $\lambda_1 = 2\lambda_2$, so x = 2 (nearest integer).

subject: Chemistry
solution:
The work functions satisfy $W_A : W_B = 1:2$.
Let $W_A = W$ and $W_B = 2W$.
Using the photoelectric equation
$KE = h\nu - W$, we have $KE_A = 6 - W$ and $KE_B = 6 - 2W$.
Given the kinetic energy ratio $\frac{KE_A}{KE_B} = 2.642$, we get $\frac{6-W}{6-2W} = 2.642$.
Solving: $6 - W = 2.642(6 - 2W)$ gives $6 - W = 15.852 - 5.284W$
So $4.284W = 9.852$ and $W = 2.3$ eV.
Thus $W_A = 2.3$ eV and $W_B = 4.6$ eV.

solution:
Statement I: Electric discharge through $H_2$ causes dissociation into excited H atoms which emit discrete frequencies (line spectrum). This is true.
Statement II: Using $ u = RcZ^2\left( rac{1}{n_1^2} - rac{1}{n_2^2} ight)$
For $He^+$ (Z=2), second Balmer line ($n_1=2, n_2=4$): $ u = 4Rc\left( rac{1}{4} - rac{1}{16} ight) = rac{3Rc}{4}$
For H (Z=1), first Lyman line ($n_1=1, n_2=2$): $ u = Rc\left(1 - rac{1}{4} ight) = rac{3Rc}{4}$
Both frequencies are equal. Statement II is true.
Both statements are true.

options: All of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi></math>-particles pass through the gold foil without decrease in speed.

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi></math>-particles pass through the gold foil deflected by small angles and with reduced speed.
solution: According to Thomson's plum pudding model, protons are diffused in a positively charged cloud (charge is not centred) and the negatively charged particles are embedded in it. So, the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">α</mi></math>- particles would deviate by small angles and due to repulsion from protons, their speed would decrease.

subject: Chemistry
solution:
Kinetic energy of electron is, 
$\mathrm{KE}=\frac{13 \cdot 6 \mathrm{Z}^2}{\mathrm{n}^2} \mathrm{eV}$ 
For the first excited state of the hydrogen atom, 
$\mathrm{n}=2$ and $\mathrm{Z}=1$ $\therefore \mathrm{KE}=\frac{13.6}{2^2}=3.4 \mathrm{eV}$

subject: Chemistry
solution:
In Isoelectronic species, number of electrons should be same
Number of electrons present in Li+= 3-1 = 2
Number of electrons present in Na+= 11-1 = 10
(A) F-=9+1=10e-
Li+=3-1=2e-
Na+=11-1=10e-
Mg+2=12-2=10e-
(B) N-3=7+3=10e-
Li+=3-1=2e-
Mg+2=12-2=10e-
Na+=11-1=10e-
(C) F-= 9+1 = 10
O2-= 8+2 = 10
N3-= 7+3 = 10
Mg2+=12-2=10
(d) Li+=3-1=2e-
Na+=11-1=10e-
O-2=8+2=10e-
F-=9+1=10e-
Hence, N3-, O2-, F-, Na+ are isoelectronic.
